vulnerabilities.name: tainted-filename
vulnerabilities.message: File name based on user input risks server-side request forgery.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

vulnerabilities.name: md5-loose-equality
vulnerabilities.message: Make sure comparisons involving md5 values are strict (use `===` not `==`) to avoid type juggling issues
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-697: Incorrect Comparison

vulnerabilities.name: phpinfo-use
vulnerabilities.message: The 'phpinfo' function may reveal sensitive information about your environment.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

vulnerabilities.name: php-permissive-cors
vulnerabilities.message: Access-Control-Allow-Origin response header is set to "*". This will disable CORS Same Origin Policy restrictions.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-346: Origin Validation Error

vulnerabilities.name: tainted-exec
vulnerabilities.message: User input is passed to a function that executes a shell command. This can lead to remote code execution.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: exec-use
vulnerabilities.message: Executing non-constant commands. This can lead to command injection.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: openssl-decrypt-validate
vulnerabilities.message: The function `openssl_decrypt` returns either a string of the decrypted data on success or `false` on failure. If the failure case is not handled, this could lead to undefined behavior in your application. Please handle the case where `openssl_decrypt` returns `false`.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-252: Unchecked Return Value

vulnerabilities.name: tainted-sql-string
vulnerabilities.message: User data flows into this manually-constructed SQL string. User data can be safely inserted into SQL strings using prepared statements or an object-relational mapper (ORM). Manually-constructed SQL strings is a possible indicator of SQL injection, which could let an attacker steal or manipulate data from the database. Instead, use prepared statements (`$mysqli->prepare("INSERT INTO test(id, label) VALUES (?, ?)");`) or a safe library.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: tainted-sql-string

Instead, use prepared statements (`$mysqli->prepare("INSERT INTO test(id, label) VALUES (?, ?)");`) or a safe library.

vulnerabilities.name: eval-detected
vulnerabilities.message: Detected the use of eval(). eval() can be dangerous if used to evaluate dynamic content. If this content can be input from outside the program, this may be a code injection vulnerability. Ensure evaluated content is not definable by external sources.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: detect-non-literal-regexp
vulnerabilities.message: RegExp() called with a `f` function argument, this might allow an attacker to cause a Regular Expression Denial-of-Service (ReDoS) within your application as RegExP blocks the main thread. For this reason, it is recommended to use hardcoded regexes instead. If your regex is run on user-controlled input, consider performing input validation or use a regex checking/sanitization library such as https://www.npmjs.com/package/recheck to verify that the regex does not appear vulnerable to ReDoS.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-1333: Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity

vulnerabilities.name: tainted-sql-string
vulnerabilities.message: User data flows into this manually-constructed SQL string. User data can be safely inserted into SQL strings using prepared statements or an object-relational mapper (ORM). Manually-constructed SQL strings is a possible indicator of SQL injection, which could let an attacker steal or manipulate data from the database. Instead, use prepared statements (`$mysqli->prepare("INSERT INTO test(id, label) VALUES (?, ?)");`) or a safe library.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: unlink-use
vulnerabilities.message: Using user input when deleting files with `unlink()` is potentially dangerous. A malicious actor could use this to modify or access files they have no right to.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
